statement.markdown: Given a staircase where each step is a certain height, and you can climb any number of steps at a time, determine the number of different ways you can climb to the top. If you cannot climb to the top, return -1. If you can climb to the top, return the number of different ways you can climb to the top. The height of each step is provided by the input array, and the maximum height is always even. You can climb from the ground to the first step for free. Your function should return -1 if it's impossible to reach the top from the ground.
statement.inputFormat: An array of integers representing the height of each step in the staircase. The first element is the height of the first step, and the last element is the height of the last step.
statement.outputFormat: The number of different ways you can climb to the top, or -1 if it's impossible to reach the top from the ground.
statement.notes: You can climb any number of steps at a time, and the maximum height of the last step is always even.

constraints.description: Number of steps in the staircase
Maximum height of the last step in the staircase

examples.explanation: The different ways to climb to the top are (0,2,4), (0,4), (2,4), and (4).
It's impossible to climb to the top from the ground, as the staircase height is not always even.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(vector<int>& heights); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int solve(int[] heights); };
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, heights: list[int]) -> int:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(vector<int>& heights) { int n = heights.size(); int[] dp = new int[n]; dp[0] = 1; for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) { dp[i] = (heights[i] % 2 == 0) ? dp[i-1] : 0; } return dp[n-1]; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int solve(int[] heights) { int n = heights.length; int[] dp = new int[n]; dp[0] = 1; for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) { dp[i] = (heights[i] % 2 == 0) ? dp[i-1] : 0; } return dp[n-1]; } };
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(heights) { var n = heights.length; var dp = new Array(n); dp[0] = 1; for (var i = 1; i < n; i++) { if (heights[i] % 2 == 0) dp[i] = dp[i-1]; else dp[i] = 0; } return dp[n-1]; }
